label: Reece Kauffman - Intuitive Surgical Clinical Sales Associate Ocala

profile.profile_text:
Name: Reece Kauffman

Current Status: Recent Graduate (0-12 months)
University: University of Colorado, Boulder
Job Type Preference: Full Time Role

Primary Roles: Associate sales representative, Sales representative, clinical sales, medical sales, orthopedic sales, trauma sales, spinal sales, prostetic sales
Secondary Roles: onsite specialist
Roles You Don't Want: pharmaceutical sales

Business Finance: Professional Services (General)
Technology: HealthTech
Healthcare Lifesciences: Healthcare Services, Hospitals and Health Systems, Medical Devices

Target Companies: Stryker, Arthrex, Zimmer Biomet, Smith and Nephew
Are You Open To Non Obvious Entry Points: Only if it is clearly connected
Preferred Locations: South Florida specifically Fort Lauderdale

Timeline: May 2026
Feedback Style: Direct & Blunt (don't sugarcoat it)

Other Concerns: Lack of sales experience, lack of connections to the industry
Strengths: Work ethic, clinical knowledge, comfortability in emergency settings

Resume Text:
REECE KAUFFMAN
(954) 494-0665 | reecekauffman@gmail.com | LinkedIn

Clinical Sales Representative candidate with active EMT experience and a B.A. in Integrative Physiology. Experienced communicating directly with physicians and medical teams in fast paced care environments and documenting clinical information accurately. Comfortable operating around medical equipment and navigating hospital settings. Brings prior B2B sales experience initiating professional conversations and building new accounts.

EDUCATION
University of Colorado Boulder
Boulder, CO
B.A. Integrative Physiology
May 2025
Relevant Coursework: Human Anatomy Laboratory, Exercise Physiology, Immunology, Endocrinology, Medical Terminology, Physiology Lab, Biology, Chemistry, Physics

CLINICAL EXPERIENCE
American Medical Response (AMR)
Bronx, NY
Special Events EMT
January 2026 – Present
Provide on site emergency medical care at large scale venues including Madison Square Garden and Radio City Music Hall
Assess patients, monitored vital signs, and administered appropriate pre hospital interventions
Document patient care reports and communicated findings to receiving medical personnel
Coordinate with event staff and supervisors to manage medical incidents in high traffic environments

Dr. Harris Gellman, Orthopedic Surgeon
Coral Springs, FL
Physician Shadow
May 2024
Present in operating room observing orthopedic surgical procedures and overall surgical workflow
Communicated directly with nurse practitioners, physician assistants, sales representatives, and scrub technicians
Observed patient consultations and treatment planning in a specialty orthopedic practice

CU Sports Medicine Facility
Boulder, CO
Physical Therapy Intern
Spring 2024
Assisted with equipment setup and patient flow during treatment sessions
Shadowed patient evaluations and rehabilitation sessions in an outpatient clinical setting
Documented observations and supported therapists with session preparation

SELECTED ACADEMIC PROJECT
Physiological Response to Music Tempo Study
Designed a controlled crossover experiment measuring heart rate, systolic blood pressure, and body temperature under varying tempo conditions
Collected and analyzed physiological data using paired t tests in R and presented findings to faculty and peers

SALES EXPERIENCE
Professional Sports Publications
Manhattan, NY
Inside Sales Representative
September 2025 – January 2026
Conducted 200 plus outbound cold calls daily to business owners and decision makers
Managed approximately 400 active B2B prospects within a rotating pipeline
Closed new advertising accounts through direct outreach and sales presentations

Merrill Lynch, Stoss Hopper and Associates
Weston, FL
Intern
June – July 2024
Supported advisors by scheduling client meetings and maintaining client communication
Generated warm leads and referrals through outreach to existing contacts

CERTIFICATIONS AND AFFILIATIONS
Certified Emergency Medical Technician | EMT Utah | July 2025
American Red Cross Volunteer

jobText:
Job highlights
Identified by Google from the original job post
Responsibilities
Please note: Candidates must live within or be willing to relocate to the Ocala, FL Area to perform the duties of this role
Essential Job Duties (Specific responsibilities and tasks an individual would be expected to perform in the role
Additional job duties may be determined by functional people manager)
Work with the Clinical Sales Manager to develop a sales strategy to drive robot utilization
Manage surgical teams through training sessions, OR integration and da Vinci® Surgery applications
Be a da Vinci® Surgery resource across all primary surgical specialties
Support regional Sales and Marketing events that create system awareness and procedure adoption
Develop a da Vinci® Surgery plan for each surgical team to ensure they can use the system unsupervised
Drive continuous expansion of the user base by working with KOLs
Drive the sales of instruments and accessories
Responsibly manage administrative tasks; sales activities, submission of expense reports, etc

Job description
Job Description
Please note: Candidates must live within or be willing to relocate to the Ocala, FL Area to perform the duties of this role.

Primary Function of Position
Surgeons worldwide are changing the way surgery is performed by utilizing our robotic surgical device to enhance their surgical precision and greatly improve patient benefits. The focus of the Clinical Sales Associate (CSA) is to clinically sell to maximize the da Vinci® Surgical System’s utilization in the Ocala, FL territory.

Essential Job Duties (Specific responsibilities and tasks an individual would be expected to perform in the role. Additional job duties may be determined by functional people manager)

Work with the Clinical Sales Manager to develop a sales strategy to drive robot utilization
Manage surgical teams through training sessions, OR integration and da Vinci® Surgery applications
Be a da Vinci® Surgery resource across all primary surgical specialties
Support regional Sales and Marketing events that create system awareness and procedure adoption
Develop a da Vinci® Surgery plan for each surgical team to ensure they can use the system unsupervised
Drive continuous expansion of the user base by working with KOLs
Drive the sales of instruments and accessories
Responsibly manage administrative tasks; sales activities, submission of expense reports, etc.
